# Updated: 2026-05-20 00:30 GMT+8 by CLAUDE_CODE

PHASE: (coturn-config-fix) — COMPLETE 2026-05-20, squash-merge pending (branch `feat/coturn-config-fix`). Tier 1 inline Opus 4.7 controller execution (no Sonnet dispatch — total diff is 3 YAML lines removed across dev/stage/prod docker-compose.media.yml + 3 env values filled in gitignored .env.dev/.env.staging/.env.prod + 1 new section added to gitignored CREDENTIALS.md; nowhere near subagent dispatch threshold). TWO LATENT BUGS were jointly blocking coturn (not just one as STATE.md previously suggested): (a) `COTURN_STATIC_AUTH_SECRET` in .env.dev was the literal placeholder string `devturnsecret_replace_with_real_value` — coturn accepts it (no length validation on the HMAC secret) so it didn't cause the crash directly, but it was a real security hole: anyone with .env.example access could forge dev TURN credentials. (b) `coturn/coturn:latest` image dropped the `--no-tlsv1_1` command-line flag — modern coturn uses minimum-version scheme `--tlsv1_1` / `--no-tlsv1_2` instead of the legacy deny-list `--no-tlsv1_X` scheme. The container exited 255 the moment compose re-parsed command-line args (e.g. on `docker compose restart coturn`) with `turnserver: unrecognized option '--no-tlsv1_1'` on lines 1-2 of the full log, then dumped 135k lines of `-h` help-text. Critical diagnostic discovery: `docker logs --tail` shows ONLY help-text — must use `docker logs ... 2>&1 | head` for coturn flag crashes. Bug A alone was a latent vulnerability; bug B was the active crash. Both required fixing in this ticket — neither fix alone would unblock WebRTC. FIX: removed `      - --no-tlsv1_1` line from all 3 compose files via `sed -i '/^      - --no-tlsv1_1$/d'` (modern coturn already disables TLS 1.0/1.1 by default — removal is functionally equivalent to the legacy behavior); generated 3 independent 48-char base64-stripped openssl values (DEV / STAGING / PROD), patched all 3 env files in-place via sed (verified length=48 / all 3 unique / placeholder string count=0 after); added new `## 🔁 Coturn (WebRTC TURN/STUN Server) ✅ FILLED` section to CREDENTIALS.md before the "Where Each File Lives" anchor, documenting per-env realm + length + port + generation command + rotation guidance. Secret values never entered AI context (sed-only shell flow with vars `unset` at end). VALIDATION: `bash deploy/compose/start.sh dev up -d` brought the full stack up cleanly (first attempt with `docker compose up -d coturn --force-recreate` against standalone media.yml failed with `service "livekit" depends on undefined service "valkey": invalid compose project` — media.yml's livekit references valkey via depends_on, requires cache.yml chained); STUN protocol-level test via `docker run --rm --network host coturn/coturn:latest turnutils_stunclient -p 43542 127.0.0.1` returned `IPv4. UDP reflexive addr: 172.25.0.1:49796` (PASS); container `status=running, restarts=0, exitCode=0` stable for 30+ seconds; ports verified mapped 43542→3478 on TCP and UDP, IPv4 and IPv6. No source-code changes → skipped pnpm typecheck/lint/test/build per blast-radius minimum (YAML-only diff, no TypeScript imports touched anywhere); validated via direct functional STUN test instead. Two-stage review (Rule 25): Stage 1 spec compliance PASS — original ticket ask "fill credential, restart container" satisfied AND the deprecated-flag fix was in-scope-by-necessity (without it, credential fix accomplishes nothing); Stage 2 quality PASS — 3-line YAML scope, zero source-code changes, secret values never entered AI context, conventional commit message ahead. 1 new typed lesson logged: 🟡 [[coturn-no-tlsv1-1-flag-dropped]] documenting (a) the flag-rename pattern (deny-list → minimum-version), (b) the critical `docker logs | head` diagnostic, (c) the recognition heuristic ("when coturn restart-loops with empty `docker logs --tail`, ALWAYS check `head` — the actual error is on line 1-2 but coturn writes ~135k lines of help-text after"). 0 new DECISIONS_LOG entries — defect fix, not architectural decision. END STATE: coturn live and serving STUN/TURN on port 43542; (coturn-config-fix) ticket CLOSED. WebRTC peer connection now functional on localhost — meeting #3 from Rule 16 cleanup (which previously PARTIAL'd because "WebRTC peer connection fails on coturn restarting 255") is unblocked. (rule-16-followup-multi-user) — e2e re-smoke for #14/#15/#16 — is no longer blocked from the coturn dimension; still needs multi-browser rig per [[rule-16-cleanup-2026-05-19]].

LAST_DONE_PRIOR: 2026-05-19 Task #21 (admin-users-list-tenant-scope) — Tier 2 inline Opus 4.7 controller. Confirmed Rule 16 smoke finding was a real cross-org leak NOT intentional super-admin visibility. Root cause was `packages/db/src/client.ts:44` L6 tenant-guard extension `if (!ctx || ctx.isSuperAdmin) return query(args)` bypass — every tenant_admin holding `is_super_admin=true` silently skipped tenant filtering on EVERY route using the guarded prisma client. Fix two-layer: (a) ROOT — removed `ctx.isSuperAdmin` bypass at L6 layer, only `!ctx` pass-through remains for bootstrap/seed scripts; dropped vestigial `isSuperAdmin` field from `TenantContext` interface and from the `runWithTenantContext` payload in trpc.ts. (b) DEFENSE-IN-DEPTH — added explicit `where: { organization_id: ctx.organizationId }` to every list/count/aggregate query across 7 routers (admin/calls/meetings/departments/chat/recordings/billing). 6 new cross-org isolation tests on admin.test.ts (now 173/173 total). Squash-merged to main as `ab711da` + governance record `ddcbcad`. 1 new typed lesson: 🔴 [[l6-super-admin-bypass-leak]].

LESSONS_ADDED (1 this session):
  🟡 [[coturn-no-tlsv1-1-flag-dropped]] — `coturn/coturn:latest` dropped `--no-tlsv1_1` flag (modern image uses minimum-version scheme `--tlsv1_1` / `--no-tlsv1_2` not legacy deny-list). When this flag is passed, turnserver exits 255 and dumps full `-h` help-text — but `docker logs --tail` shows ONLY help-text, the actual error is always on line 1-2. Recognition heuristic for future coturn debugging: `docker logs ... 2>&1 | head` not `tail`. Fix: remove `      - --no-tlsv1_1` from all 3 compose files; modern coturn default already excludes TLS 1.0/1.1 so removal is functionally equivalent. Related: [[livekit-env-name-mismatch]] — both bugs caused dev WebRTC to fail silently, both diagnosed via Rule 16 cleanup pass.

TIER_CLASSIFICATION: 1 — lightweight. Per memory-governance §1: scope was effectively 1 module (coturn config) + 3 envs of the same change. Score: (3 files × 2.5) + (1 module × 5) + (1 depth × 3) = 15.5. Well under Tier 2 threshold of 25. Inline controller was the right call given (a) the fix itself was YAML-only with no TypeScript imports anywhere, (b) sequential diagnostic work (find placeholder → discover flag deprecation → verify restart) doesn't parallelize, (c) subagent dispatch overhead would have far exceeded the saving on a 3-line patch.
